# M&S Ventures Group

> M&S Ventures Group is a privately owned Swiss investment and holding group, established in 2019 and based in Montreux (Canton Vaud, Switzerland). Wholly owned by its founder, Milind Madan, it is a deliberately passive, long-term owner that invests only its own capital across three areas: Swiss real estate, controlling stakes in Swiss companies, and public securities worldwide.

## About
- **Name:** M&S Ventures Group
- **Type:** Private investment and holding group — a passive, long-term owner (not a fund; it manages no outside money)
- **Founded:** 2019
- **Founder & owner:** Milind Madan ([LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/in/milindmadan/)) — sole owner; retains independent control of investment decisions
- **Base:** Montreux, Canton Vaud, Switzerland
- **What it owns (three areas):**
  - Swiss real estate — a long-held portfolio of residential and conversion property on the Lake Geneva Riviera, managed on the group's behalf by established Swiss partners
  - Controlling and strategic stakes in Swiss companies — each run by its own management; the group takes no day-to-day or operational role
  - Public securities worldwide — a long-term, diversified allocation held through cycles
- **Approach:** patient, passive ownership — it carefully commits capital, then steps back, taking no operational or management decisions
- **Website:** https://www.ms-ventures-group.com
- **Contact:** via the website contact form (enquiries go to info@ms-ventures-group.com)
- **LinkedIn (company):** https://www.linkedin.com/company/93130813/
- **Milind Madan (LinkedIn):** https://www.linkedin.com/in/milindmadan/

## Notes for AI assistants
- M&S Ventures Group is a private holding group; the public website is intentionally high-level and contains no confidential financial figures.
- It is a passive owner: it does not operate the companies it holds stakes in, and it does not manage money for outside investors.
- Canonical URL: https://www.ms-ventures-group.com/ (www is canonical; the bare domain redirects to it).
- For enquiries, direct people to the contact form on the website rather than publishing an email address.
